_meta.purpose: Wizard-1 validation ground truth: what an expert Open Dental schema mapping should look like. Diff target for scripts/validate_wizard.py.

source_id.notes: Direct PK on the treatment plan table.

provider_id.notes: Open Dental's treatplan table has no direct ProvNum; falls back to the planning provider on the constituent procedures (proctp.ProvNum). When a plan has procedures by multiple providers, this picks one — operator should confirm selection rule.

presented_date.notes: DateTP is the canonical 'date plan was presented' field.

accepted_date.notes: Open Dental records patient signature time as DateTSigned. Practices that don't use electronic signatures will have NULLs here even for accepted plans — flag for operator review.

declined_date.notes: Open Dental has no explicit 'declined' date column. Practices typically infer decline from 'plan not signed within X days'. Recommend post-extraction derivation rather than mapping.

expired_date.notes: Open Dental does not auto-expire treatment plans; no source column exists.

status.source_expression: CASE WHEN treatplan.DateTSigned IS NOT NULL THEN 'accepted' WHEN treatplan.TPStatus = 1 THEN 'declined' ELSE 'presented' END
status.notes: TPStatus enum: 0=Active, 1=Inactive (treated here as 'declined' because Open Dental practices most commonly mark plans Inactive after a documented patient refusal — but it can also mean expired or superseded; operator MUST confirm). DateTSigned presence is the most reliable accepted-signal.

plan_dollars.source_expression: (SELECT SUM(proctp.FeeAmt) FROM proctp WHERE proctp.TreatPlanNum = treatplan.TreatPlanNum)
plan_dollars.notes: Sum of constituent procedure fees on the plan. Excludes tax (typically 0 in dental).

procedure_category.notes: Open Dental stores ADA D-codes on proctp.ProcCode but no canonical category. The de-id tool's existing procedure_category mapping (per the v0.1 contract) would need to be applied at a higher level. Mark NULL for now and let downstream classify.

transformations.status: CASE WHEN treatplan.DateTSigned IS NOT NULL THEN 'accepted' WHEN treatplan.TPStatus = 1 THEN 'expired' ELSE 'presented' END

notes: Open Dental treatment plans don't expose a clean lifecycle — accepted/declined/expired must be derived from a combination of DateTSigned, TPStatus, and time-since-presentation. Recommend a follow-up review with the practice on their workflow conventions before relying on the status field.
GROUND TRUTH AUDIT: TPStatus=1 mapped to 'declined' rather than 'expired' (per Open Dental docs, TPStatus=1 is Inactive — the practice manually deactivated; usually post-refusal).

payer_category.notes: Open Dental stores carrier name as free text. Mapping carrier name to canonical payer_category enum requires a per-practice lookup table or transformation rules. Operator must supply the carrier-name -> category mapping.

payment_date.source_expression: (SELECT MAX(claimproc.DateCP) FROM claimproc WHERE claimproc.ClaimNum = claim.ClaimNum AND claimproc.Status = 1)
payment_date.notes: Latest claimproc.DateCP for Status=1 (Received/paid). NULL if no procs paid. Per Open Dental docs, Status=1 is the canonical 'Received' code; Status=2 is Preauth (pre-authorization, not a payment) and is a common source of analyst error.

denial_date.notes: Open Dental does not flag claims as 'denied' explicitly. Denials show up as ResponseStatus in clearinghouseslog. Practices vary in how they record outright denials vs. partial pays.

authorization_required.notes: Inferred from presence of pre-auth string. Practices that don't track pre-auth in this field will return false even when auth was used.

authorization_date.notes: No explicit auth-date column in Open Dental claim table.

denial_reason_category.notes: Denial reasons live as free text in clearinghouseslog.ResponseDetail. Mapping to canonical category requires NLP or a curated lookup.

status.source_expression: CASE WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'R' THEN 'paid' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'A' THEN 'paid' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'S' THEN 'submitted' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'I' THEN 'pending' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'H' THEN 'pending' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus IN ('U','W') THEN 'pending' ELSE 'pending' END
status.notes: ClaimStatus is a 1-char enum: U=Unsent, W=Waiting, P=Probable, S=Sent, R=Received, H=Hold, I=Sent-verified, A=Adjustment. Open Dental does not differentiate paid-in-full from partial in this field — 'partial' must be derived from amount comparison.

pre_verified.source_expression: EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM insverify WHERE insverify.FKey = claim.PatNum AND insverify.VerifyType = 0 AND insverify.DateLastVerified >= DATE_SUB(claim.DateService, INTERVAL 90 DAY))
pre_verified.notes: Pre-verification heuristic: any insverify row for the patient within 90 days before service. The 90-day window is a guess; practices' verification refresh intervals vary.

transformations.status: CASE WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'R' THEN 'paid' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus IN ('U','W') THEN 'pending' WHEN claim.ClaimStatus = 'S' THEN 'submitted' ELSE 'pending' END
transformations.payer_category: /* practice-specific carrier-name -> canonical-payer-category lookup table required */

notes: Carrier-name to payer-category requires a practice-specific lookup. Many practices have only a few carriers; suggest the wizard prompt the operator for a mapping table.
Pre-verification window is a guess; should be a configurable parameter.
GROUND TRUTH AUDIT: payment_date corrected from claimproc.Status=2 to claimproc.Status=1 (the canonical Received code per Open Dental docs). Status=2 is Preauth.
GROUND TRUTH AUDIT: status CASE expanded to cover H/I/A enum values.

provider_id.notes: For provider-grain rows, ProvNum is direct.

chair_id.notes: For chair-grain rows, join schedule -> scheduleop -> operatory and group by OperatoryNum. The mapping should produce a UNION ALL of provider-grain and chair-grain rows.

productive_hours.source_expression: (SELECT SUM(appointment.AptLength) / 60.0 FROM appointment WHERE appointment.ProvNum = schedule.ProvNum AND DATE(appointment.AptDateTime) = schedule.SchedDate AND appointment.AptStatus = 2)
productive_hours.notes: Sum AptLength for completed (AptStatus=2) appointments matching the row's grain. Note: AptLength is in minutes in modern Open Dental but legacy installs use 5-minute increments — verify with practice.

notes: Output is a UNION ALL of provider-grain (via schedule.ProvNum) and chair-grain (via scheduleop.OperatoryNum) rows.

source_id.notes: Each payment may have multiple splits; the canonical row is at split-grain.

payment_source.notes: Need UNION ALL with claimpayment (insurance) and adjustment (writeoff) tables. This mapping covers patient payments only. Operator should review the full UNION expression.

payer_category.notes: Patient-paid splits are 'self_pay' by definition. Insurance splits would map differently (via UNION'd claimpayment branch).

notes: Full Extension D coverage requires UNION ALL of paysplit (patient), claimpayment (insurance), and adjustment (writeoff). The mapping above only covers the patient branch.

staff_role.notes: Open Dental does not store non-provider staff roles in the PMS. Staff timekeeping typically lives in payroll software (Gusto, ADP, etc.); recommend an integration there for role-grain rows.

scheduled_hours.notes: Hours scheduled. Same source as Extension C but different grain.

productive_hours.source_expression: (SELECT SUM(appointment.AptLength) / 60.0 FROM appointment WHERE appointment.ProvNum = schedule.ProvNum AND DATE(appointment.AptDateTime) = schedule.SchedDate AND appointment.AptStatus = 2)
productive_hours.notes: Same as Extension C productive_hours. AptLength units may need verification per install.

hourly_rate.notes: provider.HourlyRate is populated only when practices use Open Dental's payroll module — many do not. Operator should verify before relying on this column.

notes: Provider-grain only; staff-role grain unmappable from PMS alone.

recall_due_date.source_expression: (SELECT MIN(recall.DateDue) FROM recall WHERE recall.PatNum = patient.PatNum AND recall.IsDisabled = 0 AND recall.DateDue >= CURDATE())
recall_due_date.notes: Soonest open recall for the patient. Practices may have multiple recall types per patient (prophy, perio, etc.); we pick the soonest.

referral_source_category.notes: patient.ReferredBy is free-text in many installs and a FK to definition (category=referral source) in others. Mapping the source value to the canonical category vocab requires a per-practice lookup. Operator must supply the mapping or set this to NULL.

notes: Extension F adds columns to existing patients_raw rows; treat the mapping as a JOIN-on-patient_source_id rather than a new file.
